events.text: Mary Davis, 62-year-old Hispanic female, Current smoker (1 ppd). Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.
Diagnosed with High-grade serous carcinoma of the Right ovary. Clinical staging: T3cN1M0, AJCC Stage IIIC. ICD-10: C56.9. Stage IIIC high-grade serous ovarian cancer with BRCA1 mutation.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.

Underwent primary debulking surgery: Total abdominal hysterectomy, bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy, omentectomy, and optimal cytoreduction (residual disease < 1 cm). Estimated blood loss 250 mL. Length of stay 3 days. Uncomplicated postoperative course.
Pathology specimen received for surgical pathology.
Clinical visit for postoperative follow-up.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic: Carboplatin AUC 6 + Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 nausea. Managed with ondansetron 8 mg q8h PRN and dexamethasone 4 mg daily.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic: Carboplatin AUC 6 + Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic: Carboplatin AUC 6 + Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 neuropathy. Dose reduction of neurotoxic agent by 25%. Gabapentin 300 mg TID started.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic: Carboplatin AUC 6 + Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Developed grade 2 neuropathy. Dose reduction of neurotoxic agent by 25%. Gabapentin 300 mg TID started.
PET/CT performed for restaging after first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic.
Clinical visit for results review after first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic.
Initiated second-line platinum-based chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1,8 q3w. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated second-line platinum-based chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1,8 q3w. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Developed grade 2 nausea. Managed with ondansetron 8 mg q8h PRN and dexamethasone 4 mg daily.
Initiated second-line platinum-based chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1,8 q3w. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated second-line platinum-based chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1,8 q3w. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Initiated second-line platinum-based chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1,8 q3w. Cycle 5. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated second-line platinum-based chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1,8 q3w. Cycle 6. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 6 assessment.
Developed grade 2 thrombocytopenia. Platelets 65 K/uL. Dose delayed one week. Recovered to 120 K/uL.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for restaging after second-line platinum-based chemotherapy.
Clinical visit for results review after second-line platinum-based chemotherapy.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.

documents.text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Mary Davis
MRN: patient_4fe95d2bca83
Date of Study: 2023-06-15

CLINICAL INDICATION: High-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: Complex cystic and solid mass in the right adnexa measuring 10.5 cm. Omental caking present. Moderate ascites. Peritoneal carcinomatosis.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Complex adnexal mass with peritoneal carcinomatosis and ascites, concerning for advanced ovarian malignancy.

Electronically signed by: Jennifer Walsh, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Mary Davis
MRN: patient_4fe95d2bca83
Specimen ID: S2023-50984
Date of Procedure: 2023-06-16
Date of Report: 2023-06-20

SPECIMEN: Right ovary, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
High-grade serous carcinoma, poorly differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 6.5 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.5 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
PAX8: Positive
WT1: Positive
P53: Aberrant (overexpression)
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Mary Davis - Right ovary" is a gray-white tissue fragment measuring 6.5 x 4.5 x 3.2 cm. The cut surface reveals a friable gray-white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A8.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a poorly differentiated neoplasm composed of nests of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and irregular nuclear contours. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows chronic inflammation.

Electronically signed by: James Park, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Medical Oncology

Patient: Mary Davis
MRN: patient_4fe95d2bca83
Date: 2023-06-22
Provider: Dr. Amanda Foster

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Davis is a 62-year-old female who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed high-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary, IIIC. Patient presented with symptoms prompting workup. Stage IIIC high-grade serous ovarian cancer with BRCA1 mutation. Pathology confirmed high-grade serous carcinoma. Molecular testing has been sent. Patient reports palpable mass noted by patient.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- chronic kidney disease stage 2

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Right knee arthroscopy (age 45)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Former smoker (quit 10 years ago)
- Alcohol: Social drinker (2-3 drinks/week)
- Occupation: Office manager
- Lives with alone

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Sister with ovarian cancer at age 55.

ALLERGIES: Sulfa drugs (hives)

MEDICATIONS:
- None

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: joint pain, night sweats, dizziness, mild cough. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 137/76, HR 93, RR 17, Temp 98.2F, SpO2 98% on RA, Wt 77.5 kg
General: Well-nourished, alert, in no distress.
HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Mucous membranes moist.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally.
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly.
Extremities: No peripheral edema. Good pulses bilaterally.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 8.8, Hgb 11.3, Plt 156, Cr 0.82

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
62-year-old female with IIIC high-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary. initial oncology consultation.

1. High-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary, Stage IIIC
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend primary debulking surgery
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - PICC line to be placed prior to treatment
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Helen Park, MD
Medical Oncology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Mary Davis
MRN: patient_4fe95d2bca83
Date of Report: 2023-07-02
Specimen: Right ovary (Core needle biopsy)
Diagnosis: High-grade serous carcinoma
Panel: Guardant360 CDx (468 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 6.8 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  BRCA1 p.E1836fs (c.5503delA) - Frameshift (Exon 22) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  NF1 p.R1947* - Nonsense (Exon 39)
  TP53 p.C176Y - Missense (Exon 5)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  CDK12 p.T826A - VUS
  ARID1A p.D1850N - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - BRCA1 pathogenic variant: FDA-approved PARP inhibitors include olaparib, niraparib, rucaparib.
  - Platinum sensitivity expected.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 454x coverage.
Tumor content: 24%.

Electronically signed by: Andrew Patel, MD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Mary Davis
MRN: patient_4fe95d2bca83
Specimen ID: S2023-76935
Date of Procedure: 2023-07-16
Date of Report: 2023-07-18

SPECIMEN: Right ovary, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
High-grade serous carcinoma, well differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 1.6 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.5 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
PAX8: Positive
WT1: Positive
P53: Aberrant (overexpression)
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Mary Davis - Right ovary" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 1.6 x 1.1 x 0.8 cm. The cut surface reveals a indurated gray-white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A5.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a well differentiated neoplasm composed of nests of atypical cells with abundant cytoplasm and prominent nucleoli. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows minimal reactive changes.

Electronically signed by: James Park, MD
Department of Pathology

FOLLOW-UP NOTE - Gynecologic Oncology

Patient: Mary Davis
MRN: patient_4fe95d2bca83
Date: 2023-07-24
Provider: Dr. Carlos Mendez

CHIEF COMPLAINT: postoperative follow-up

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Davis is a 62-year-old female with IIIC high-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary who presents for postoperative follow-up. Patient reports feeling well overall. Appetite has improved.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: joint pain, insomnia. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 126/82, HR 71, RR 19, Temp 97.9F, SpO2 97% on RA, Wt 85.4 kg
General: Alert, oriented, well-appearing, in no acute distress.
HEENT: NCAT, EOMI, PERRL. Oral mucosa moist without lesions.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear bilaterally. No rales or rhonchi.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, mildly tender in RUQ. No rebound. Normoactive bowel sounds.
Extremities: Trace bilateral lower extremity edema. Warm and well-perfused.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 3.1, Hgb 13.2, Plt 305, Cr 0.82

IMAGING:
Most recent imaging reviewed.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
62-year-old female with IIIC high-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary. postoperative follow-up.

1. High-grade serous carcinoma - postoperative follow-up
   - Treatment well tolerated, continue
   - Will check labs prior to next cycle
2. Symptom management
   - Pain well controlled on current regimen
3. Follow-up in 3 weeks

Electronically signed by: Carlos Mendez, MD
Gynecologic Oncology

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: Complex cystic and solid mass in the right adnexa measuring 7.7 cm. Omental caking present. Moderate ascites. Peritoneal carcinomatosis.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Complex adnexal mass with peritoneal carcinomatosis and ascites, concerning for advanced ovarian malignancy.
2. Overall assessment: Stable disease per RECIST 1.1.
